PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY PETITIONER PREFERRED MEAL SYSTEMS, INC AS TO RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED
APPLICATION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE, DECLARATION FILED BY PETITIONER PREFERRED MEAL SYSTEMS, INC
PROOF OF SERVICE FILED BY REAL PARTY IN INTEREST REVOLUTION FOODS, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE WAS A PARTNER AT MORRISON & FOERSTER FROM 1997 THROUGH 2/7/2005 PRIOR TO HER APPOINTMENT TO THE BENCH. THE REAL PARTY IN INTEREST IS REPRESENTED BY MORRISON & FOERSTER. SHE KNOWS SUSAN MCCORMICK, A FORMER PARTNER AT THE FIRM AND DOES NOT HAVE ANY CONTACT WITH HER. SHE DOES NOT VIEW THIS AS A BASIS FOR HER RECUSAL TO BE FAIR & IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE APPLICATIONS OF JOYCE TONG OELRICH AND WILLIAM M. WEISBERG TO APPEAR AS COUNSELS PRO HACE VICE FOR THE PETITIONER WAS GRANTED IN OPEN COURT WITHOUT OPPOSITION. THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING THE ISSUANCE OF A PEREMPTORY WRIT - THE WRIT IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE COURT AFTER REVIEWING THE PROCEEDINGS FOR ABUSE OF DISCRETION FINDS THAT SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE SCHOOL DISTRICT'S FINDINGS THAT RFI WAS A RESPONSIVE BIDDER AND THE RFI BID WAS RESPONSIVE. THE SCHOOL DISTRICT DID NOT VIOLATE THE EVALUATION CRITERIA BY PERMITTING RFI TO RESPOND TO THE PROTEST. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED DENYING PREFERRED MEAL SYSTEMS, INC'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE FILED BY RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT

THE REAL PARTY IN INTEREST IS REPRESENTED BY MORRISON & FOERSTER. SHE KNOWS SUSAN MCCORMICK, A FORMER PARTNER AT THE FIRM AND DOES NOT HAVE ANY CONTACT WITH HER. SHE DOES NOT VIEW THIS AS A BASIS FOR HER RECUSAL TO BE FAIR & IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE APPLICATIONS OF JOYCE TONG OELRICH AND WILLIAM M.
